purpose: Top-level `yolo` CLI entry: builds the Typer app, registers all subcommands (defined in sibling cli/* modules), rewrites argv so `yolo -- cmd` routes to `yolo run -- cmd`, sets kitty-tab/tmux-pane "JAIL" indicators with atexit restore, and prints the startup banner. Also owns the version-string discovery stack, the shared rich Console singleton, the path/runtime constants used by every other cli module, and the host-side TTY proxy that intercepts Ctrl-Z (0x1A) before it reaches the jail so Claude Code can't wedge PID 1.

files.role: Typer app + top-level callback (global options, rich help text), ~200 lines of re-exports from all cli/* submodules (test/monkeypatch surface), main() entry: YOLO_INVOCATION_CWD chdir, argv `--`→`run` rewrite, kitty/tmux indicator setup + atexit restore, app() dispatch
Shared rich Console singleton (standalone to avoid circular imports)
tmux window/pane-border and kitty tab-title/color jail indicators (save/set/restore via subprocess to tmux/kitten), startup banner to stderr
run_with_proxy(): pty-based host TTY proxy; raw mode, ^Z self-suspend, SIGCONT re-raw, SIGWINCH forwarding, SIGHUP/SIGTERM teardown hook, non-TTY plain-Popen fallback
Version discovery: YOLO_VERSION env > git describe (normalized) > setuptools-scm src/_version.py > importlib.metadata; --version eager callback; container-baked YOLO_VERSION via `<runtime> inspect`

Constants: IS_LINUX/IS_MACOS, SUPPORTED_RUNTIMES=(podman,container), NATIVE_RUNTIMES=(macos-user,), image names, ~/.local/share/yolo-jail tree, user config path, /run/yolo-services, cgroup-delegate.sock / journal.sock names

public_surface.name: yolo (console script)
public_surface.detail: pyproject [project.scripts] yolo = "src.cli:main". Global options on root callback: --network (default "bridge"), --new, --profile, --dry-run (macos-user only), --version (eager, prints "yolo-jail <ver>" and exits 0). No subcommand → invokes `run` with forwarded options. Root help is rich-markup text (user-visible doc).
public_surface.consumers: host users, agents in-jail via generated wrapper (~/.yolo-shims/yolo and flake.nix yoloCli both do `from src.cli import main; main()` against /opt/yolo-jail)

public_surface.name: argv rewrite: `yolo -- CMD` → `yolo run -- CMD`
public_surface.detail: main() scans sys.argv; if `--` present and nothing before it is in the hardcoded subcommand set {init, init-user-config, config-ref, check, run, ps, doctor, loopholes, broker, builder, macos-setup, macos-teardown, macos-unshare, macos-fix-permissions}, inserts "run" before `--`. Go port must keep this exact set in sync with registered commands.

public_surface.name: subcommand registration
public_surface.detail: app.command(): init, init-user-config, config-ref, prune, check, run (allow_extra_args + ignore_unknown_options), ps, doctor, macos-setup/teardown/unshare/fix-permissions; add_typer: loopholes, broker, builder. Implementations live in other module groups; this group only wires dispatch.

public_surface.name: python -m src.cli
public_surface.detail: __main__.py calls main(); flake.nix in-jail wrapper uses `python3 -c "from src.cli import main; main()"` under uv with --with typer --with rich --with pyjson5>=2.0.0

public_surface.name: YOLO_INVOCATION_CWD
public_surface.detail: main() pops it from os.environ and chdirs there (best-effort) before anything else — jail-side shim compensation so workspace/config resolution uses the user's real CWD. Popped, so never propagates to children.

public_surface.name: YOLO_VERSION
public_surface.detail: Read by _git_describe_version() as highest-priority version override (host CLI sets it before spawning containers so in-jail banner matches host); _container_baked_yolo_version() greps it out of `<runtime> inspect .Config.Env`.

public_surface.name: KITTY_PID / KITTY_WINDOW_ID / TMUX / TMUX_PANE
public_surface.detail: Terminal detection. Priority: KITTY_PID set AND TMUX unset → kitty tab path; else tmux path. KITTY_WINDOW_ID picks `--match id:<id>` (else `recent:0`).

public_surface.name: SM_PROJECT
public_surface.detail: Overrides project label (else cwd basename) used in kitty tab title / tmux pane border text.

public_surface.name: terminal side-band protocol
public_surface.detail: subprocess to `tmux` (rename-window, show-option -pt, set-option -pt/-put, show-window-option, set-window-option automatic-rename off, display-message -p, batched restore with `;` separators) and `kitten @` (get-tab-title/set-tab-title/set-tab-color --match id:N). Sets title "🔒 JAIL <project>", pane border " 🔒 JAIL <dir> ", red border/tab colors (#cc0000/#880000); restore on atexit resets to saved values (title fallback "bash", colors "none").

public_surface.name: startup banner (stderr)
public_surface.detail: `yolo-jail <ver> | <sys.platform>/<machine> | <runtime> | <cname>` — ver becomes `yolo-jail <ver> (attached to jail built at <jail_ver>)` when container-baked YOLO_VERSION differs. Optional second line `Resource limits: a, b, c`. Users paste this into bug reports; keep byte-identical.

public_surface.name: version string normalization
public_surface.detail: git describe `v0.1.0-3-gabcdef1-dirty` → `0.1.0+3.gabcdef1.dirty`; exactly-on-tag → `0.1.0`; dirty-on-tag `v0.1.0-dirty` → `0.1.0.dirty` (note: NO `+` in that case); leading `v` stripped. Fallback chain: YOLO_VERSION env → git describe (cwd=repo root, 5s timeout) → src/_version.py (setuptools-scm) → importlib.metadata("yolo-jail") → "unknown".

public_surface.name: <runtime> inspect for baked version
public_surface.detail: `<runtime> inspect --format '{{range .Config.Env}}{{println .}}{{end}}' <cname>` with 3s timeout; parses YOLO_VERSION= line; any failure → None (never fatal).

public_surface.name: tty_proxy signals
public_surface.detail: Installs handlers while proxying (restores previous on exit): SIGWINCH → read TIOCGWINSZ from host stdin, TIOCSWINSZ onto master pty; SIGCONT → tty.setraw(stdin) (re-raw after fg); SIGHUP/SIGTERM → restore cooked termios, run on_terminate() (e.g. podman stop), os._exit(128+signum); self-delivered SIGTSTP (default disposition) on ^Z byte after restoring cooked mode. Non-TTY path installs NO handlers.
public_surface.consumers: host shell job control, window-close teardown

public_surface.name: run_with_proxy exit codes
public_surface.detail: Returns wrapped child's exit code verbatim (both TTY and non-TTY paths); 128+signum via os._exit on SIGHUP/SIGTERM; FileNotFoundError propagates if cmd missing (after closing pty fds).

public_surface.name: TTY byte protocol
public_surface.detail: Host stdin raw (tty.setraw); 4096-byte chunks; 0x1A splits chunk: pre-bytes forwarded, proxy self-suspends, post-bytes flushed after SIGCONT; child gets slave pty as stdin+stdout+stderr; initial winsize copied from host TTY; select() 0.1s timeout poll loop; drain of master after child exit so final output isn't truncated; stdin EOF → close master (sets sentinel -1).

public_surface.name: paths.py constants
public_surface.detail: GLOBAL_STORAGE=~/.local/share/yolo-jail (+ /home /mise /cache /containers /agents /build), USER_CONFIG_PATH=~/.config/yolo-jail/config.jsonc, JAIL_HOST_SERVICES_DIR=/run/yolo-services, CGD_SOCKET_NAME="cgroup-delegate.sock" (MUST match entrypoint + YOLO_SERVICE_CGROUP_DELEGATE_SOCKET), JOURNAL_SOCKET_NAME="journal.sock", JAIL_IMAGE=localhost/yolo-jail:latest, JAIL_IMAGE_SHORT=yolo-jail:latest (Apple Container can't parse localhost/ prefix), SUPPORTED_RUNTIMES=(podman,container), NATIVE_RUNTIMES=(macos-user,).
public_surface.consumers: every cli/* module, entrypoint expectations, daemons

third_party_deps.used_for: app construction, options, eager --version callback, ctx.invoke forwarding, rich_markup_mode help
third_party_deps.go_equivalent: cobra (or urfave/cli); help text must be re-rendered — rich markup tags don't translate

third_party_deps.used_for: Console singleton (styled output used by all subcommands)
third_party_deps.go_equivalent: fatih/color or charmbracelet/lipgloss; or plain ANSI

side_effects: spawns tmux/kitten subprocesses; changes user's terminal tab title/colors and tmux pane borders/window name; atexit restore
tty_proxy: puts host TTY in raw mode, installs/restores 4 signal handlers, opens/closes a pty pair, spawns the runtime process, self-delivers SIGTSTP, may os._exit
version: runs `git describe` (cwd=repo root, 5s timeout) and `<runtime> inspect` (3s timeout)
python_specifics: cli/__init__.py re-exports ~150 private symbols from every submodule so tests can `from cli import X` and patch('cli.foo') — this monkeypatch identity is the de-facto test contract; a Go port breaks it, so parity must move to process-level tests
dual import identity: package is importable as both `cli` and `src.cli` (conftest/agent_registry rely on it)
typer ctx.invoke(run, ...) forwards root-callback options into the run command; comment warns un-forwarded params would keep truthy OptionInfo defaults — subtle Typer artifact, don't replicate
lazy circular import in version.py (`from . import _resolve_repo_root` inside function) with fallback to Path(__file__).parent^3 as repo root
importlib.metadata / setuptools-scm src/_version.py fallbacks — Go should use ldflags -X baked version instead, but must keep YOLO_VERSION env override and git-describe normalization identical
tty.setraw mutates termios; cooked attrs captured once and restored with TCSANOW at suspend/exit/signal-teardown

os.kill(getpid(), SIGTSTP) with DEFAULT disposition to stop whole process incl. daemon threads (doc: chosen over raise_signal; POSIX stops all threads). Go: runtime installs no SIGTSTP handler by default so syscall.Kill(pid, SIGTSTP) works, but any signal.Notify(SIGTSTP) would break stop semantics — must leave disposition default

signal handlers call tty.setraw/tcsetattr and os._exit(128+signum) from handler context; Go equivalent is a signal goroutine — ordering with the pump loop must be reasoned out (Python handlers interrupt the main thread; goroutines race)
deliberately NO start_new_session on Popen — child must share the session so bash job control and pty allocation work (doc: setsid broke -it)
on_started runs on a daemon thread post-spawn (releases workspace lock while proxy pumps); SIGTSTP stopping all threads is what makes this race-free — Go needs an explicit story

select() loop with 0.1s timeout + proc.poll() polling; stdin-EOF sets master=-1 sentinel expecting select to skip it, but Python select() actually raises ValueError on negative fd — near-dead code path (raw TTY rarely EOFs); do NOT copy literally, decide correct EOF semantics and test
atexit.register(restore) for terminal cleanup — Go needs defer in main + signal-path invocation
emoji (🔒) and rich markup in user-visible strings; banner uses sys.platform values ('linux','darwin') — Go GOOS matches but keep exact banner text

seams.name: tty-proxy binary
seams.contract: exec-style: proxy(argv, on_started?, on_terminate?) → child exit code. Behavior: non-TTY stdin → plain spawn inheriting stdio; TTY stdin → pty pair, raw mode, 0x1A intercept + self-SIGTSTP, SIGCONT re-raw, SIGWINCH → TIOCSWINSZ, SIGHUP/SIGTERM → restore TTY + teardown + exit 128+n, drain master on child exit. Hardest part: on_started/on_terminate are Python closures (lock release, `podman stop`); a standalone Go binary needs them expressed as sub-commands/argv hooks (e.g. --on-terminate 'podman stop <cname>'), or the Go proxy stays a library until run_cmd itself ports
seams.swap_mechanism: env var (e.g. YOLO_TTY_PROXY=/path/to/go-proxy) checked in run_with_proxy: if set, os.exec/subprocess the Go binary with the wrapped argv and hook args; else Python path. Call sites: run_cmd.py:1383,1439,3000 and macos_user.py:1288
seams.parity_test: scripted pty harness (Python pexpect or script(1)): run `<proxy> -- cat` on a real pty; assert ^Z stops the proxy (WIFSTOPPED, child cat got no signal), fg/SIGCONT resumes and pre/post-^Z byte split is exact; SIGWINCH propagates (check stty size inside); SIGTERM → exit 143 + termios restored; `echo hi | proxy -- cat` → 'hi', exit 0; exit-code passthrough for exit 7

seams.name: whole-CLI front door
seams.contract: `yolo` binary: argv rewrite rules, subcommand set, global flags, --version output `yolo-jail <ver>`, terminal indicator setup/restore, then dispatch. A Go front-end can parse/dispatch and exec `python -m src.cli <argv>` (with YOLO_INVOCATION_CWD semantics) for not-yet-ported subcommands
seams.swap_mechanism: replace the installed console script / the two wrapper generators (flake.nix yoloCli, entrypoint/scripts.py generate_yolo_wrapper) to point at the Go binary; Go binary execs Python for unported commands
seams.parity_test: golden tests: `yolo --version`, `yolo --help` (text will differ — pin new golden), `yolo -- echo hi` argv rewrite (observe child argv via strace/echo), `yolo check --no-build` passthrough unchanged; banner line byte-compare with YOLO_VERSION pinned

seams.name: version discovery
seams.contract: pure function: (YOLO_VERSION env, git describe output, baked version, metadata) → normalized string per the rules above; plus `<runtime> inspect` env grep
seams.swap_mechanism: trivially portable library; or a `yolo-version` helper binary compared side-by-side during transition
seams.parity_test: table test over describe outputs {v0.1.0, v0.1.0-dirty, v0.1.0-3-gabc-dirty, abc1234 (no tag)} → identical strings from both implementations; run in a repo checked out at a tag / dirty / detached

seams.name: terminal indicators
seams.contract: exact tmux/kitten argv sequences (set/save/restore) listed in public_surface; all best-effort/silent-failure
seams.swap_mechanism: port with the front door; A/B by recording subprocess argvs (PATH-shim fake tmux/kitten that logs calls) under both implementations
seams.parity_test: fake tmux/kitten shims write invoked argv to a log; diff logs Python vs Go for setup+restore in tmux env, kitty env, YOLO_NO_TMUX=1, non-TTY

existing: tests/test_cli_unit.py: TestRunWithProxy — non-TTY fallback (exit code, on_started sync call, exception swallow, on_terminate unused on non-TTY); banner formatting incl. jail-version mismatch/match/missing; version git-describe + pkg-metadata fallback

tests/test_cli_commands.py: TestMainFunction — kitty-vs-tmux priority, argv `--`→run insertion, no-rewrite when subcommand present; TestTmuxRenameWindow/TestTmuxSetupJailPane/TestKittySetupJailTab — no-op guards (no TMUX, YOLO_NO_TMUX, not-a-tty), set+restore call shapes, kitten failure tolerance
tests/test_state_separation.py + test_cli_commands.py exec-path tests patch cli.run_cmd.run_with_proxy (proxy is mocked, not exercised)
gaps: PARTIAL coverage of the real TTY proxy loop (CORRECTED 2026-07-17): TestRunWithProxy covers the non-TTY fallback and TestProxyTeardownSignal (tests/test_cli_unit.py:5125, commit b09c9f1) drives the real loop behind a real pty asserting SIGHUP/SIGTERM -> teardown callback + exit 128+n. Still uncovered: ^Z byte-split math + pending-flush after resume, bash job-control (outer shell sees Stopped; wrapped child receives no signal and keeps running), SIGWINCH forwarding, SIGCONT re-raw, master drain-on-exit, stdin-EOF/-1 sentinel

version normalization edge cases (dirty-on-tag → `0.1.0+dirty` (CORRECTED 2026-07-17: earlier map text said `0.1.0.dirty` no-`+`, copied from a stale comment; the code at version.py:99-108 emits `0.1.0+dirty`); hash-only describe) untested
tmux restore batching (single `tmux cmd \; cmd` invocation) argv shape untested
test_coverage.assessment: Dispatch and no-TTY paths are well covered via monkeypatching; the highest-risk code (raw pty proxy) has zero automated coverage. Before porting, build a pty-driven black-box harness (pexpect/script) against the Python proxy first, then reuse it as the Go parity gate. The mock-based tests won't survive a Go port — the parity surface must be process-level (argv, env, exit codes, subprocess call logs).

porting_hazards: tty_proxy is the top hazard: raw termios save/restore, self-SIGTSTP with default disposition (Go runtime must not Notify SIGTSTP), signal-handler-context TTY restore + os._exit(128+n), pty winsize ioctls, and the child sharing the proxy's session (no setsid — doc records that setsid broke podman -it). Go's signal model (goroutine, not interrupting handler) changes suspend/resume ordering; needs careful design + real-pty tests

on_started/on_terminate are in-process Python closures crossing the proxy boundary (lock release, podman stop with captured cname) — cannot swap the proxy to a Go binary without redesigning these as argv-passed hooks or porting run_cmd's call sites simultaneously

the ~150-symbol re-export block in cli/__init__.py is the test suite's patch surface; incremental porting silently breaks dozens of @patch('cli.X') tests per module moved — plan test migration per module
argv rewrite subcommand set is hardcoded and must stay in lockstep with registered commands (adding a Go subcommand without updating the set makes `yolo newcmd -- x` misroute to run)
Typer help text is rich-markup and part of the UX (agents read it); Go help output will differ — decide whether help text is in or out of parity scope

version normalization quirk: dirty-on-exact-tag yields `0.1.0+dirty` (CORRECTED 2026-07-17: an earlier stale code comment claimed `0.1.0.dirty` with no `+`; the code emits `0.1.0+dirty` — generate goldens from observed output, never comments)
stdin-EOF path sets master=-1 then would pass it to select() → ValueError in Python; effectively dead on real TTYs. Decide intended behavior; don't transliterate
banner `sys.platform` values ('linux'/'darwin') happen to equal GOOS — verify platform.machine() vs runtime.GOARCH naming (x86_64 vs amd64, arm64 vs aarch64) for byte-identical banner

kitty/tmux restore relies on atexit — but the SIGHUP/SIGTERM handler in tty_proxy calls os._exit, which SKIPS atexit: terminal indicators are NOT restored on kill/window-close today; replicate (or consciously fix) in Go
emoji + UTF-8 in tab titles/pane formats — keep exact bytes for parity diffing of tmux/kitten argv logs

paths.py CGD_SOCKET_NAME comment records a real regression (renamed socket broke the cgroup delegate silently) — socket filenames are cross-image contracts baked into the Nix entrypoint; constants must be shared/generated, not re-typed, in the Go tree
notes: Files: /workspace/src/cli/{__init__,__main__,console,terminal,tty_proxy,version,paths}.py; design doc /workspace/docs/ctrl-zand-the-tty-proxy.md (actual path: /workspace/docs/ctrl-z-and-the-tty-proxy.md) is required reading for the proxy port. Recommended order: (1) paths constants as a shared Go package generated or cross-checked against Python, (2) version library with table-driven parity tests, (3) pty harness for the Python proxy, (4) Go proxy behind YOLO_TTY_PROXY env swap, (5) front-door dispatch last since it must exec Python for unported subcommands. Console singleton is trivial but its rich styling leaks into every subcommand's output — output-parity scope decision needed early.
